reviews.review_msg: For a 2 hour lecture there was only about 30 slides- not bad. Get help from TA's. In class, don't focus too much on what's written on slides, listen to what he says. What he says is most likely going to be on test. First test is simple, next one is challenging. I suggest studying in advance.

All I can say to the people that have to take STAT 201, TAKE IT WITH BRAD. Wait until summer. IT'S WORTH IT. Practice problems are almost identical to the exams and as long as you get it checked by the TAs or him, YOU'RE GOLDEN. Lectures are a bit boring due to his voice being monotone but is very knowledgeable and genuinely wants your to succeed.

If you want to learn everything simply, take Stat 302 with Brad. Listen to his lectures properly, take the necessary notes, and do the R exercises, and you can get at least a B+.
 Lectures are not boring. Just listen to each word he says and visualize a little bit in your head about the topic he is discussing.
He doesn't really address the exam material in class. Just do the practice questions he posts as R exercises, and you'll do good.

THE GOAT!! If you have him, KEEP HIM! While his lectures are a bit boring & his voice is monotonous, the midterms and final exams were EZPZ. All the questions were from the exercises, but nothing drastic. It isn't code-heavy compared to other profs' HW. Just go on Zoom imo PLEASE TAKE BRAD FOR STAT 201! I got an F the 1st time with Rachel Altman.

Super chill class. 3 quizzes worth 20% and final worth 40% (worst quiz gets dropped if you do better on the final) the quizzes and final are pretty much the same as the exercises so definitely do those. he lets you make a cheat sheet for everything. would definitely recommend taking this class with him!!

If lectures don't make sense, read the textbook and make notes for each chapter (very helpful for making your cheatsheets). I would still recommend watching lectures b/c things that are not in the textbook are mentioned in class and can appear on exams. Very straightforward prof, so I would definitely recommend taking STAT 201 with Dr. McNeney.

lectures are pretty boring. 80% of class wouldn't show up for them since attendance wasn't mandatory. he speaks in a very monotonous voice, so it's rlly easy to fall asleep during lectures if ur tired. 3 quizzes worth 20% each, final worth 40%. problems on quiz/final pulled directly from the exercises on canvas so would 100% recommend u do them.
Grading is only based off quizzes and the final. Studying for this class is relatively easy as the tests have questions similar (if not the same) to the R exercises.
pretty chill prof, quizzes are straight forward, lets you create cheat sheets, majority of the questions are coming pretty much straight from the practice  questions he gives you

Grading is 20% per quiz, 40% final (also worst quiz dropped if u do better on final). If you do the lectures and exercises, the quizzes are very straightforward. Same concept for the final, Brad's exams are very fair. Super chill guy.
He is the best professor in statistics. If you can take a course with him, you won't regret it.
this guy bet me one grimace shake if i scored 90% on the stats201 final. glad to say that i enjoyed that grimace shake. happy birthday grimace.
we went to wendys and got a bag of jbcs. coolest prof ever
Quizzes are relatively straightforward and he allowed a cheat sheet. Make sure to do the exercises, as they are similar to questions you'll encounter on the quizzes/final.
Because of Brad, I was able to time travel and order the Travvy Patty from McDonald's. Shoutout Brad

I was eating at McDonalds when all of a sudden Brad pulled up with R exercises. He demanded my food because he was so hungry after teaching. I was confused because I saw him eating during the entire lecture. I haven't eaten in weeks!!
mmmmmm brad
Really easy teacher

Do his practice homework and you will finish in the class with an A, his exams are almost the same as his practice assignments. He's a really caring and nice prof and answers a lot of questions when you ask. If yall have him, take this course , trust

Prof was very nice and made mid terms and finals really easy cause they were exactly like the homework. Lectures really confused me and I had a hard time understanding the topics through his explanations. Most of my learning was done through YouTube videos. If you do the homework and understand it the course will he easy for you.
recorded all lectures, slides are a little confusing sometimes, but exams were very straightforward, he uses homework questions on exams since he does not release answer keys

He was very good professor. He considered my condition and he likes to talk to students. He is very kind to get answers for my questions. If another professor teaches this course, I can not get good grade.

Boring lecture, hard to follow. He hand writes notes for this course, and students are expected to copy every thing that he writes down, which left us no time to digest what he says. He describes concepts by simply showing us their mathematical formula, and expect students to understand,I personally don't understand a single word of his explanation

Good prof. Helpful through email & always asks class if they have questions. Class is organized well & teaching is pretty good. Didn't use Monday class (nice) & got vids instead. Sometimes went thru topics fast in the first few chapters. Quizzes r open book, long, &time crunched so study. Final was 3x as long but fair. Guided us in R. Useful class!

I think professor McNeney did a fairly good job with this course. The class was well organized and he teaches the concepts well enough. I found the way he explained some concepts to be confusing, especially concerning the MARS algorithm for our final project.

Brad is super caring and very helpful. The class was difficult for those who don't have a programming background, and the group project required a ton of work. However, Brad will help you always if you are willing to ask questions and come to his office hours. In the end, I learned a lot!

Professor Mcneney is probably one of the "better" professors for stat. He does talk really fast during lectures but he does try to reexplain concepts more clearly when asked to. He does often make mistakes for quiz questions so he would either omit it or change some grading.

Great class design (Book/GIT/Slides/Assignments). Focus on understanding how to interpret and use statistical learning. Brad covered more material than any prof I've ever had. Tests were hard but fair. He always took questions and tried to explain anything with which someone was confused. Shame only 20% of the class actually showed up to lecture.

I honestly do not even know what happened in that class. After the first week everything was based off of a statistical software that he expected you to be fluent in. He understands the material, however it did not translate to being able to teach it well. Reads off the slides which make no sense at all.

This course was absolutely disastrous. For people who have a good Stats background, this is no problem. For someone (like me) who doesn't have good background in "R", which is the language you use for this course, he doesn't even go over the basics and glances over the main ideas. Awful marker and his exams go TOO detailed to test big idas. AVOID.
The most awful experience, extremely arrogant and not helpful at all. Reading over the slides is not teaching. Very disappointed.

He knows the material well, writes excellent exams, and gives has clear expectations for exams/assignments. No surprises, no "gotchas". Also has lectures recorded and gives full lecture notes. No real complaints, definitely one of the top statistics teaching faculty.

Seems like a great guy and professor for Statistics students, however, as a HSCI student, I was lost and felt like he and other stats students in the class were always 10 steps ahead of us students who aren't as well versed in math/statistics. Again, good guy, cares about stats, but STAT sfu courses not t all that accessible. Had to teach myself

Had him for STAT 305/STAT 341. Brad should set the standard for teachers everywhere. Great notes, fair but challenging tests, is organized, has office hour at a reasonable time and makes lecture useful though not mandatory. He can answer 99% of student questions and really knows the material. He even knows he is boring->rare jokes. Beware his MCQs.
Posts full lecture notes and recorded lectures. Good explanations and clearly lays out expectations for exams.

Really good professor. His assignments are really good. The best part of the assignments is that they can be finished in one go if you are dedicated enough. His midterms are also student friendly. In totality, I would say that Brad is an excellent professor. I wish more professors follow his style of teaching.

One of the best profs I've had at SFU. Brilliant lecture material all on github so it's easy to go through DURING class while he lectures and tweak code yourself if you want. Assignments and tests were straightforward. Honestly guy deserves massive props for the way he ran that course

Brad is an excellent professor . He really takes lot of efforts to make the lectures in a practical manner. He is really accessible to his office hours and his assignments are pretty easy and makes us to understand concepts in a practical way. His exams are really great. if you understand the concept, you can score full.

A very solid choice for this course.  Makes the work fairly straightforward and there are no surprises in terms of his exams and homework assignments.  I actually learned some practical/useful things from the class, which is rare.
Brad is a really good prof,  he made the things easy to understand, and clear lecture note .  the assignments and practices are very helpful to the exam. But speaks too fast.
He is really disorganized and is always late to lecture. He spends most of lecture sitting down and just repeating what's on the slides but isn't good at making the material accessible.
not bad for a survey course he made it really clear whats we will be test on before exams.  if u  understand the  materials on practice exams then u should be fine
Don't  understand what he is talking in class. He speaks so fast. Reading textbook is more helpful. Terrible handwriting. Midterm 1 is easy, midterm 2 is hard.

Brad is a great person, you can tell. His ability to make the material simple for students is low. Midterms were simple, but the averages were outrageously high, so his final was impossible. Cross-reference marking with someone else-high % of errors from the stats workshop. Difficult class for students without strong stats background.

It's not difficult if put in the effort to understand the material. It helps to understand it during homework so you won't have to cram for the exams and to not rely too much on the cheat sheet. Not hard to get a B+ or A- just don't slack and go to class. He talks fast but if you keep up his lectures are pretty helpful
Brad is a nice prof. he is very helpful and good at teaching.
Brad is such a nice prof! He truly cares about his students and listens to their opinions. Exams are easy and the same as his practice questions! He also tells you what questions will be on the exam!

Very helpful if you go to his office hours. TA's are pretty much useless so his notes is really your backbone + his office hours. Hate the textbook but if you put the time into reading it, it's not TOO bad. HW was similar to the test so make sure you understand the HW!!
Stats with this guy was so easy. As long as you do know how to do the homework and do the practice exams you're good to go. Not hard to get an A.

From the first moments of this class it was loaded with jargon and very few plain language explainations or examples. However the assignments wern't bad and the exams were really easy if you did the practice exams. The text book is awful (and $200 with suspiciously few used copys around for a book published in the 80's). All in all an okay class.

The class is really boring (what do you expect it's stats). That being said Brad tries his best to make the material interesting but it doesn't help that his voice is somewhat monotone. His exams are REALLY easy as long as you do his practice exams provided. Also allowed a cheat sheet in the midterm and final :)

I thought he was a fair prof. The lecture material is boring and he tried cracking jokes to make it interesting. The assignments are in groups and you are allowed a cheat cheat for the final and midterm. He tells you what to expect; his exams are easy!! I definitely recommend him as a prof

I found Brad to be a really good professor. Although the lecture material is very dry, he tells you what will be on the exam and what you don't need to know. midterm was  easy, average was 82. He's also very helpful and nice when it comes to not understanding something. Lecture notes are also very clear and concise.

You're allowed a cheat cheat on his midterm and final. Assign - 20%, midterm - 30%, final - 50%. If you screw up your midterm, final can be worth 80%..... material is awfully dry. Final was harder than expected.

After a mind-bending lecture, a scared student asks: "Uh, will this be on the exam?" McNeney: "Oh no, you won't come across this until 3rd year." *_* I would rather eat glass than take a course with him again. Cold and boring and unnecessarily difficult.
Like a true statistician, he is totally unashamed of his nerdyness! :p

This teacher definitely knew his stuff, BUT didn't provide any examples in his notes or lectures that will allow you to understand homework and exam questions. The textbook this semester was awful. But practice exams are quite similar to real ones. How I managed to get an A I don't know.
He doesn't give examples of the material he is presenting, very hard to relate to a practical sense of learning.
I don't really understand the materials he teach. But he is very patient and easy approach. Easy class.

This course is pretty difficult, the textbook is terrible. Even though the pro is less organized and dry, but his notes is helpful and all the exams are open-book. Do use the workshop and TAs there are wonderful. The pro. gave all the key points before exam, and answer questions through class caucus quite in time.

he sounds like he's bored, but maybe because it's the summer semester. but he's a good prof and he's SUPER DUPER EASY! (maybe because it's the summer semester?) midterm was sooo easy, just wish i had studied more to get a great mark. read his notes and you'll do absolutely fine. good attitude to students and funny sometimes :P
This guy is unorganized and dry

Not an easy class, although it should have been.  Maybe it was the fact it was incredibily boring or that his notes really didn't help.  Wish I had learned more.  Assignments were easy but were a lot diff than the tests.  Makes a good attempt with a boring subject but overall I do not think he is a good prof.  Good luck staying awake.
No Comments

Very poor lectures. I wanted to learn more than I did from this course. Very disappointing. Not challenging at all, unless you have a problem with calculating means... He should have taught the chi square test because it is common in biological research.
Very boring material, but a very helpful and nice guy.
really easy..there's no excuse for not doing well in his class..
Super easy, take it as a GPA booster
Nice teacher whose assignments are very fair. There is potential for the course material to be interesting, but his lectures are dry.
boring lectures but very helpful and nice.
Doing his best with an incredibly boring and easy course.  He helps people a lot on the Caucus site.  Seems like a nice guy, but needs more personality.
Nice guy, boring class
Need a little sleep? Come to this class. Nice guy, dry personality & materia
